mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter signals easier financial conditions, lowers discount rates, and lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average after 3-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign relationship flips for more than one consecutive 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still accelerating, investors should price continued policy restraint and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rises for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if inflation acceleration does not strengthen the effect of high FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and softer demand, which should compress earnings expectations and weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly rises in UNRATE are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the effect disappears once unemployment has risen for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS after a prior decline indicates improving nominal sales demand and better revenue momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises month-over-month after at least one prior monthly decline
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS rebound events do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal is not distinguishable from unconditional return noise.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, industrial output is improving faster than housing supply, indicating a broadening cyclical upswing that should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread between INDPRO and HOUST changes does not improve 20-day SPX return predictability, or if the interaction term has no incremental value over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A negative payrolls impulse in PAYEMS signals weakening labor income and slower consumption growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal remains negative during all payroll drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession rebound, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS reflects stronger real output and nominal spending, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior release
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS gains do not improve 60-day SPX outcomes versus either series alone, or if the joint signal fails after recessions.

mechanism_narrative: During a commodity-driven inflation shock, rising CPIAUCSL with falling INDPRO signals stagflationary pressure, compressing margins and real activity, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates while INDPRO declines for 2 straight months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-up/output-down condition does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if CPIAUCSL alone explains the effect equally well.
